message:
fix: atomize cleanup coordinator locks, auto-remove terminal jobs, deterministic scheduler ordering, and minor correctness fixes

Several independent correctness and robustness fixes across the runtime:

cancel/symbol_cancel.rs — CleanupCoordinator::cleanup() previously took
the handlers lock, released it, then took the pending+completed locks.
This created a window where a concurrent cleanup call could see the
pending set but find the handler already removed, silently losing
symbols. Now all three locks (handlers -> pending -> completed) are
acquired atomically in a single scope, eliminating the interleaving
race.

net/worker_channel.rs — FinalizeCompleted now calls remove_job()
instead of transitioning to Completed state and leaving the entry in the
tracking map. This is the counterpart to the remove_job() method added
in a prior commit — terminal jobs are now actually cleaned up, closing
the memory leak.

io/copy.rs — Missing `steps += 1` in the copy loop's yield-back path.
Without this, the step counter would not advance when the copier yields
to avoid starving other tasks, potentially causing the progress guard
to misreport the copy as stalled.

messaging/jetstream.rs — Replaced Box::pin(sub.next(cx)) with
std::pin::pin!(sub.next(cx)) in the timeout path. The Box allocation
was unnecessary since the future is immediately awaited and never
stored; stack-pinning avoids the heap round-trip on every poll.

runtime/scheduler (global_injector.rs, priority.rs) — Added a final
.then_with(|| other.task.cmp(&self.task)) tiebreaker to all three Ord
implementations (TimedTask, SchedulerEntry, TimedEntry). Previously,
entries with identical priority/deadline AND identical generation were
unordered, making BinaryHeap iteration non-deterministic across runs.
The task ID tiebreaker ensures fully deterministic ordering, which is
required for DPOR exploration reproducibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
